title: Mexican Street Corn Salad
intro: Mexican street corn (elote) is built on contrast: blistered corn, creamy coating, sharp lime, salty cheese, and chili heat. This version keeps that structure while tightening texture distribution and restoring a clear, punchy finish.

ingredientGroups.title: Crunch Layer
items: ¼ cup roasted, salted pepitas, finely chopped

ingredientGroups.title: Flavor Build
items: ¼ cup finely crumbled cotija cheese

stepGroups.title: Char the Corn
items: Preheat a grill or heavy pan over high heat. Toss corn with olive oil and salt. Cook, turning occasionally, until kernels are deeply charred in spots but still juicy, about 8 to 10 minutes. Remove and let cool slightly.

stepGroups.title: Cut and Structure
items: Stand each ear upright and slice kernels off the cob. Use the back of the knife to lightly scrape the cob lightly, just enough to release a small amount of corn milk for body without loosening the salad.

stepGroups.title: Build the Dressing
items: In a large bowl, combine Greek yogurt, mayonnaise, garlic, lime juice, and lime zest. Mix until smooth and lightly fluid, not thick.

stepGroups.title: Combine
items: Add warm corn to the dressing and toss thoroughly so it lightly coats every kernel.

stepGroups.title: Layer Flavor
items: Add cotija, cilantro, chili powder, smoked paprika, cayenne, salt, and pepper. Toss again.

stepGroups.title: Tame the Onion
items: Toss diced red onion with lime juice and let sit 5 minutes to take off the raw edge.

stepGroups.title: Distribute the Crunch
items: Add half of the chopped pepitas to the salad and fold thoroughly so every bite has some texture.

stepGroups.title: Finish
items: Fold in the onion and fresh cilantro. Transfer to serving bowl, then sprinkle remaining pepitas evenly over the top.

stepGroups.title: Final Dust
items: Lightly dust the surface with chili powder, smoked paprika, and a pinch of salt for a clear, immediate flavor hit.
